global.title: Powerhouse uses GraphQL Schema Definition Language to define both state schemas and operation inputs
global.content:
Powerhouse uses GraphQL SDL as the specification language for document models. This serves two purposes:

- **State schema**: `type` definitions describe the document's data structure (e.g., `type TodoListState { items: [TodoItem!]! }`).
- **Operations schema**: `input` definitions describe the payload for each operation (e.g., `input AddTodoItemInput { text: String! }`).

GraphQL SDL was chosen because it provides a clear, structured schema that supports custom scalar types (like `OID`, `Currency`, `DateTime`), enforces non-null constraints, and serves as a machine-readable specification that both humans and AI agents can understand. This is the foundation of Powerhouse's "Specification Driven Design & Development" approach.
global.description: GraphQL SDL is the shared specification language for document structure (types) and operation payloads (input types).
